matchup: Los Angeles Dodgers @ Los Angeles Angels

pre_bet_model_projection.edge_classification: thin positive EV with strong K ceiling

dodgers_offense_vs_soriano.run_creation_note: Dodgers generated six runs despite only three team hits through the snapshot, driven heavily by walks, HBP, sacrifice fly, and inherited runners scoring after Soriano exited.

angels_offense_context.notable_driver: Jorge Soler doubled twice; Jo Adell drove in both Angels runs.

liveflow_timeline.read: Initial edge was thin but playable due to K ceiling and projected median of 6.
Small negative CLV versus entry, not enough to trigger cashout or cancellation. Anti-burn posture: hold only, no add.

liveflow_timeline.line: 5.1 IP, 1 H, 6 ER, 6 BB, 6 K, 92 pitches
liveflow_timeline.read: Ticket cashed exactly at 6 Ks despite major command volatility. K ceiling held, but run prevention collapsed through free passes and inherited runners.

what_worked: Median projection landed exactly on 6 Ks
K ceiling remained intact even with poor command
Anti-burn correctly avoided adding after small negative CLV
Leash was sufficient for Soriano to reach the K threshold
what_to_modify: Apply stronger command-volatility penalty when BB risk is elevated
Separate K ceiling edge from overall pitcher quality edge
Flag 6+ as playable but fragile when strike percentage projects below stability zone
For command-volatile pitchers, avoid ladders beyond median unless live CSW/strike rate confirms
model_evaluation.patch_recommendation: KEEP K ceiling logic, ADD COMMAND_VOLATILITY_PENALTY for ladder expansion and stake sizing

confidence_level: High for K threshold evaluation, moderate for pitcher quality/run prevention
notes: This was a successful K-edge call but not a clean pitcher-performance call. Soriano cleared 6+ Ks while allowing six earned runs and six walks. Log as a stuff-over-command strikeout hit with command volatility requiring future stake/ladders adjustment.
